pi_items.source: Medical literature verification. Petrous bone and high-density dental structures start around 1500 HU. Confirmed in CT 0051 data.
Direct analysis of CT Cranium DICOM dataset (v3.0). Maximum HU value in dataset is 3033. Metadata value 3071 is conservative upper bound.
Reasonable estimate based on DICOM data volume analysis. Estimated ~1500-8000 triangles for marching cubes surface of 1500-3071 HU structures.
Conservative upper bound. Task description notes 'full skull is usually >200k', so 150k is appropriate maximum for 'dense structures only'.
Conservative lower bound. Estimated volume of 1500-3071 HU structures is ~28.7 mL, well above 2.0 mL minimum.
Conservative upper bound. Full CT cranium volume is roughly 1000+ mL; dense structures only (~28.7 mL) are well below 250 mL limit.

privileged_info_summary: Data source is real: CT Cranium (CT 0051) sample dataset from InVesalius v3.0 GitHub release (https://github.com/invesalius/invesalius3/releases/download/v3.0/0051.zip). DICOM dataset contains 108 slices from SIEMENS SOMATOM PLUS 4 scanner with voxel dimensions 0.479 x 0.479 x 2.0 mm. The target HU range 1500-3071 is medically appropriate for capturing petrous bone, dental enamel, and metallic artifacts. Actual maximum HU in dataset is 3033 (just below claimed 3071). Estimated volume of structures in 1500-3071 HU range is approximately 28.7 mL, well within task bounds of 2.0-250.0 mL.
